Yelp Open Dataset: Exploratory Data Analysis

Jerry Luo

2026-05-08

1. Introduction

1.1 The Yelp Open Dataset

The Yelp Open Dataset is a publicly available subset of Yelp’s platform data, It was originally put together for the Yelp Dataset Challenge which is a chance for students to conduct research or analysis on Yelp’s data and share their discoveries. In the most recent dataset you’ll find information about businesses across 8 metropolitan areas in the USA and Canada.

1.2 Dataset Format

The dataset is distributed as six JSON files:

File Contents
business.json Location, attributes, categories, hours
review.json Full review text, ratings, and vote counts
user.json Profiles, social graph, compliments
checkin.json Checkins on a business
tip.json Tips written by a user on a business
photo.json Not used

JSON is not suitable for data analysis, as the files must be fully parsed before any processing.

1.3 Conversion to SQLite

The dataset was pre-processed via a Python script that reads each JSON file and inserts scalar fields into a SQLite database. Fields whose values are JSON objects or arrays were dropped or converted into comma separated list.

SQLite was chosen because: - Memory usage is significantly reduced. - Querys are usually faster.

2. Setup

library(RSQLite)
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(usmap)

con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), "yelp_dataset/yelp.db")
dbListTables(con)
## [1] "business" "checkin"  "review"   "tip"      "user"

7.2 Tip Volume Over Time

8. Reviewer Rudeness by State

We can investigate how rude the users are in a given states by checking how often a one-star review is given.

8.1 Summary by States

##    state total_reviews n_businesses one_star_n one_star_rate mean_stars
## 1     IL         51832         2145      10556        0.2037     3.5121
## 2     DE         70302         2265      14074        0.2002     3.5068
## 3     NJ        260897         8536      51506        0.1974     3.5571
## 4     NV        430678         7715      76978        0.1787     3.7251
## 5     AZ        431708         9912      75214        0.1742     3.6944
## 6     ID        157572         4467      26137        0.1659     3.7601
## 7     FL       1161545        26330     189588        0.1632     3.7618
## 8     TN        614388        12056      92871        0.1512     3.7604
## 9     PA       1598960        34039     241130        0.1508     3.6988
## 10    MO        502385        10913      75066        0.1494     3.7341
## 11    AB        109436         5573      16212        0.1481     3.5689
## 12    IN        489752        11247      68970        0.1408     3.7893
## 13    CA        348856         5203      42849        0.1228     3.9600
## 14    LA        761673         9924      88302        0.1159     3.8815

8.2 One-Star Review Rate

8.3 Mean Star Rating

A complementary view — darker blue indicates that reviewers in that state tend to award higher scores on average.

8.4 One-Star Rate by States

8.5 Conclusion

The one-star rates do differ from state to state, though not by a lot. Illinois has the highest one-star rate. This can suggest that the residents are more critical commenter, or service quality are poorer.

8.6 Limitation

  • Reviews from bots are accounted.
  • Limited coverage in the states. Mostly concentrated in some metro areas.
  • Other factors influence rating decisions.
